rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE BECK VS. TONG HO HSING INDUSTRIAL CO LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Quash Subpoena And For Protective Order For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BECK'S Motion To Quash Subpoena And For Protective Order For Production Of Business Records. The motion is continued to December 17 to be heard in Department 301 at 9:00 a.m. on the discovery calendar. The Court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. The reply indicates that an opposition was served, but the Court has no courtesy copy of that pleading and there is no record of an opposition ever being filed. The opposing party shall file a copy of its opposition and provide a courtesy copy with department 301 by December 6 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL H. WERMER et al VS. SALLY A. UNTERBERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Enforce Settlement And Appoint A Referee And For Costs And Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, Line 3. PLAINTIFFS PAUL WERMER, CAROL BROWNSON'S Motion To Enforce Settlement And Appoint A Referee And For Costs And Attorneys' Fees. The motion to enforce the settlement and appoint a referee is denied without prejudice. Neither side is entitled to attorney's fees and defendant is not entitled to liquidated damages. Both sides have breached the cooperation requirements of §7.10 of the Access Agreement, which is incorporated into the parties' settlement agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, Line 5. PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S Motion To Tax Costs. The Court grants and denies the motion to strike in part. The Court strikes $2992.95 (the expert witness fees and delivery charges) from item 13. Plaintiff does not present any evidence explaining how those costs were reasonably necessary to the litigation and the Court exercises its discretion under CCP 998(c)(1) to deny the claimed expert fees. The Court denies the motion to tax as to the remaining costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KATHY BOUSSINA AS ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF et al VS. SHEILA MANCHESTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained as to the second and fifth causes of action. As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege the property that Defendant took, secreted, appropriated, obtained, or retained. As to the fifth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Auto insurance is neither a good nor service as those terms are defined by the Consumer Legal Remedies Act. (Civil Code section 1761.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KATHY BOUSSINA AS ADMINISTRATOR OF THE ESTATE OF et al VS. SHEILA MANCHESTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted in part. The motion is granted as it relates to claims for punitive damages. Ms. Rodgers settled her claim with Defendant and Defendant's delay to that point is not malicious, oppressive or fraudulent. Paragraphs 23, 34, 38, and 51 of the First Amended Complaint are struck. Paragraph 3 of the Prayer of the First Amended Complaint is struck. The motion is granted as it relates to Plaintiffs' prayer for prejudgment interest. Defendant did not cause any person injury to Plaintiffs or Ms. Rodgers. Paragraph 9 of the Prayer of the First Amended Complaint is struck. The motion is denied as it relates to the fifth cause of action. It is improper to move to strike an entire cause of action. The motion is off calendar as it relates to the second cause of action in light of the court's ruling on the demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DIANE LINEHAN VS. BAXTER INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Joinder) In The Mtn To Compel Further Answers And Documents From Session One Of Pltf'S Deposition Filed On Behalf Of Natalia Petrosova, M.D.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST MARY'S MEDICAL (Joinder) In The Motion To Compel Further Answers And Documents From Session One Of Plaintiff's Deposition Filed On Behalf Of Natalia Petrosova, M.D. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Produce the joinder document(s) at the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC. A VS. ALBERT L BOASBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANTS ALBERT BOASBERG, SCOTT MANDELL, ARTHUR FONDEN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not, and cannot, allege that its name or likeness was used "on or in products, merchandise, or goods, or for purposes of advertising or selling, or soliciting purchases of, products, merchandise, goods or services¿" as required by Civil Code section 3344. The demurrers to the sixth and eighth causes of action are overruled. Plaintiff has properly pled these claims. The demurrers to the seventh, ninth, tenth and twelfth cause of action on statute of limitations grounds are sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead how it was unable to discover its claim earlier despite reasonable diligence. Any and all demurrers based on other grounds not specifically addressed lack merit and are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: OMAR ALEMAN VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, Line 18. DEFENDANT GEORGE HERBST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. ROWEN MADAMBA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 3, 2012, Line 19. DEFENDANT ROWEN MADAMBA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion is continued to January 9, 2013. Plaintiff shall provide a courtesy copy of the opposition with department 302 by December 7 per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff shall please attach a cover letter to those papers indicating the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/AJR)
